message:
fix(plan): flatten nested joins when comparing obligation finalize order

The side-condition checker for plan rewrites compared obligation-bearing
children at the immediate join level only. When an associativity rewrite
restructured Join(Join(A,B),C) into Join(A,B,C), the checker saw different
child lists and incorrectly rejected the rewrite as unsafe — even though
the left-to-right finalize order of obligation-bearing leaves was identical.

Renamed obligation_bearing_children() → obligation_finalize_order() and
replaced the flat filter with a recursive collect_obligation_finalize_order()
that walks through nested Join nodes to extract the full leaf-level
obligation sequence. This makes the checker associativity-aware: any join
tree restructuring that preserves left-to-right obligation order is
correctly recognized as safe.

New test: finalize_order_allows_flattening_nested_join_with_obligations
verifies that Join(Join(obl:a,obl:b),obl:c) ≡ Join(obl:a,obl:b,obl:c).

Minor formatting adjustments to assert! macros and inline comments to
satisfy rustfmt.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
